rows.source_ref: Robopip, "Risk Adjustments: SMA Crossover Pullback Forex System," Babypips.com Art of Automation blog, 2015-10-09, https://www.babypips.com/blogs/art-of-automation/forex-sma-20151009
rows.strategy_name: SMA Crossover Pullback (Robopip) with breakeven trail
rows.concept: Dual-SMA crossover trend system entered on a pullback after the cross, managed with a fixed 150-pip stop and 300-pip target that switches to a trail-to-entry (breakeven) once price moves 150 pips in the trade's favor.
rows.eligibility_reason: Deterministic, pure-MQL5 SMA-crossover pullback system with hard 150-pip stop / 300-pip target and breakeven trail (FTMO/DXZ-compatible, no ML); but this addendum only fully states exit/risk management — the entry SMA periods and timeframe live in prior blog posts, not here.
rows.notes: This PDF is a Q3-2015 risk-adjustment follow-up, not the base system spec; only the added trailing stop (BE at +150 pips) is new here. Entry SMA periods and timeframe not stated in this doc — timeframe H4 inferred from the 150/300-pip stop/target scale, treat as a design choice to recover. Generic MA-crossover FX trend variant, likely correlated with existing trend/swing sleeves; author explicitly rejected a ranging-market filter and any fundamentals. Pages 4-9 are partner ads and site boilerplate.

notes: Text extracted via pdfplumber (Read tool lacked poppler); strategy content confined to pages 3-4, remainder is advertising/boilerplate.
